# Constraints Intel

All constraints below have SPEC precedence unless noted as PRD-origin. SPEC >

## CONSTRAINT-001: Color token system — exact values required
type: schema
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md (PRD)

The following CSS custom properties must be defined exactly as specified.
Deviating from these values (e.g. substituting a similar hex) breaks the AAA
contrast guarantees established during the design audit.

Light mode (:root):
  --cream:        #f5f3f0   (app bg, primary surface)
  --cream-2:      #ece8e3   (secondary surface, raised cards)
  --cream-3:      #e7e2dc   (tertiary surface, subtle wells)
  --ink:          #292524   (primary text, dark accents)
  --ink-2:        #4a4641   (secondary text, body copy)
  --ink-3:        #544e48   (tertiary text, captions)
  --ink-4:        #8a8380   (quaternary text, hints)
  --ink-5:        #d6d3d1   (disabled text, dividers)
  --amber:        #f59e0b   (brand primary, CTAs, focus accent)
  --amber-d:      #7c2d12   (amber on light bg, AAA-safe)
  --amber-l:      #fef3c7   (amber soft tint, callouts)
  --blue:         #1e3a8a   (info accent, AAA on cream)
  --purple:       #5b21b6   (secondary accent)
  --green:        #14532d   (success accent)
  --red:          #991b1b   (error / destructive accent)
  --rule:         rgba(0,0,0,.10)   (borders, dividers)
  --surf-1:       rgba(255,255,255,.4)
  --surf-2:       rgba(255,255,255,.55)
  --surf-3:       rgba(255,255,255,.7)
  --focus:        var(--amber)
  --focus-ring:   0 0 0 3px rgba(245,158,11,.45)

Dark mode (body.dark):
  --cream:        #1c1917
  --cream-2:      #292524
  --cream-3:      #44403c
  --ink:          #f5f3f0
  --ink-2:        #d6d3d1
  --ink-3:        #b8b3af
  --ink-4:        #aaa39e
  --ink-5:        #44403c
  --amber-d:      #fbbf24
  --blue:         #7cb8fb
  --purple:       #bfa3fb
  --green:        #4ade80
  --red:          #fb8888
  --rule:         rgba(255,255,255,.10)
  --surf-1:       rgba(255,255,255,.04)
  --surf-2:       rgba(255,255,255,.06)
  --surf-3:       rgba(255,255,255,.10)
  --focus-ring:   0 0 0 3px rgba(251,191,36,.5)

Vivid variants (same in both modes — used for charts, badges, status):
  --amber-vivid:  (implicitly #f59e0b — same as --amber light value; matches DataGrid usage)
  --blue-vivid:   #3b82f6
  --purple-vivid: #8b5cf6
  --green-vivid:  #22c55e
  --red-vivid:    #ef4444

Note: --amber-vivid is referenced in ds-datagrid.jsx as var(--amber-vivid) for
priority medium dots. The README lists it in the vivid set implicitly (same hex
as --amber light). Must be defined as a standalone token.

## CONSTRAINT-002: Typography — font families and scale
type: nfr
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md (PRD)

  --display: 'Archivo', system-ui, sans-serif   (display headlines, card titles)
  --mono:    'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace   (labels, eyebrows, numbers)
  (body):    'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif   (page default, body copy)

Type scale (px values are design reference; use rem in implementation):
  60–80px   hero titles
  36–48px   section titles
  22–28px   page titles
  15–18px   UI labels, button text, card titles
  13–14px   body copy
  11–12px   captions, monospace labels
  9–10px    uppercase eyebrows, badges (letter-spacing: .08–.12em)

Letter-spacing:
  display text:         -0.02em to -0.035em (tightened)
  monospace eyebrows:   +0.05em to +0.12em (loosened)
  body/UI text:         0 (default)

Line-height:
  display:  0.95–1.1
  UI:       1.4–1.5
  body:     1.55–1.65

Fonts are Google Fonts (free). Embed via @font-face or existing font loader.

## CONSTRAINT-003: Spacing scale
type: schema
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md (PRD)

12-step scale in 4px increments:
  --space-1:  4px   --space-2:  8px   --space-3:  12px  --space-4:  16px
  --space-5:  20px  --space-6:  24px  --space-7:  28px  --space-8:  32px
  --space-9:  36px  --space-10: 40px  --space-12: 48px  --space-16: 64px

Common layout uses: 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32px for component padding and gaps.

## CONSTRAINT-004: Border radius scale
type: schema
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md (PRD)

  --radius-sm:   4px
  --radius-md:   8px
  --radius-lg:   12px
  --radius-xl:   16px
  --radius-pill: 999px

Usage mapping:
  Buttons:        8px (--radius-md)
  Cards:          12px (--radius-lg)
  Chips/badges:   999px (--radius-pill)
  Modals:         14–16px (between --radius-lg and --radius-xl)
  ColorPicker:    14px container, 10px gradient area

## CONSTRAINT-005: Shadow scale
type: schema
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md (PRD)

  --shadow-1: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.05)    (subtle lift)
  --shadow-2: 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,.08)   (card hover)
  --shadow-3: 0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,.12)  (floating elements)

Usage: shadows used sparingly; prefer borders + cream surfaces. Deep modal
shadow: 0 16px 48px rgba(0,0,0,.18) (exceeds scale — intentional for dialogs).

## CONSTRAINT-006: Motion token contract
type: nfr
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md (PRD)

  --ease-out:     cubic-bezier(.2,.8,.2,1)   (entrances, hovers)
  --ease-in-out:  cubic-bezier(.65,0,.35,1)  (reversible transitions)
  --ease-spring:  cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1)  (playful pops, sparingly)
  --dur-1: 120ms   (hover, focus, color shifts)
  --dur-2: 180ms   (component state changes)
  --dur-3: 240ms   (modals, tooltips entering)
  --dur-4: 360ms   (page transitions, large layout shifts)

  prefers-reduced-motion: reduce must cap animations at 200ms or instant.

## CONSTRAINT-007: CSS class name conventions (inter-component contract)
type: protocol
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md (PRD)

The following CSS class names are referenced across multiple SPEC files and form
a cross-component protocol. Any component that renders these elements must apply
the correct class to remain visually consistent:

  ds-btn           base button
  ds-btn (variants): primary, secondary, ghost, destructive, amber, dark, danger
  ds-btn-sm        small button modifier
  ds-btn-xs        extra-small button modifier
  ds-icbtn         icon-only button (used in Pagination, CommandPalette)
  ds-input         text input
  ds-input-wrap    input with prefix/suffix slot (used in ColorPicker inline variant)
  ds-label         form field label
  ds-field         field wrapper (label + input)
  ds-toggle        toggle switch (with .on modifier)
  ds-chip          chip/tag
  ds-badge         status badge (with modifier: done, progress, todo, urgent,
                   upcoming, passed, pending)
  ds-card          card surface
  glass            translucent glass surface (backdrop-filter: blur)
  ds-dropdown      dropdown menu container
  ds-dropdown-item dropdown menu item
  ds-sidebar-item  sidebar nav item
  ds-table         table element
  ds-checkbox      checkbox (with .checked modifier)
  ds-page-btn      pagination page button (with .active modifier)
  ds-overlay       modal/dialog overlay
  ds-cmd           command palette container
  ds-cmd-input-wrap command palette input row
  ds-cmd-input     command palette search input
  ds-cmd-body      command palette results area
  ds-cmd-group     command palette group header
  ds-cmd-item      command palette result item
  ds-breadcrumb    breadcrumb nav container
  ds-bc-item       breadcrumb item (with .active for current page)
  ds-bc-sep        breadcrumb separator
  ds-accordion-hd  accordion header row
  ds-popover       context menu / popover container
  ds-popover-item  context menu item
  ds-skel          skeleton loading placeholder (pulsing animation)
  ds-kbd           keyboard shortcut display
  ds-relative-time relative time span

## CONSTRAINT-008: DataGrid dependencies on shared components
type: api-contract
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-datagrid.jsx (SPEC)

DataGrid renders the following shared components internally. These must exist
before DataGrid can be implemented:
  - ds-badge (with modifier classes: upcoming, done, passed, pending)
  - ds-checkbox (with .checked modifier, inline SVG checkmark)
  - ds-table (table element class)
  - ds-btn (with xs and danger modifiers for bulk-action bar)
  - ds-page-btn (with .active modifier for pagination footer)

## CONSTRAINT-009: ColorPicker state contract
type: api-contract
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-colorpicker.jsx (SPEC)

ColorPicker maintains three pieces of internal state:
  - color: string — current selected hex color (source of truth for previews)
  - hex: string — text field value (may be partial/invalid during typing)
  - opacity: number (0–100)

Hex input updates color only when input matches /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/ (full 6-digit hex).
Preset and strip clicks update both color and hex simultaneously.
The opacity bar does NOT affect hex value — only the alpha display field.

## CONSTRAINT-010: ConfirmDialog container constraints
type: api-contract
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-confirmdialog.jsx (SPEC)

  - Fixed width: 360px
  - Background: rgba(255,255,255,.97) — NOT var(--cream); intentionally always-light
    to match the modal glass pattern for maximum contrast behind overlay
  - Backdrop filter: blur(14px)
  - Border-radius: 14px
  - Shadow: 0 16px 48px rgba(0,0,0,.18)
  - This means ConfirmDialog does NOT use body.dark token recoloring internally —
    it is always rendered against the overlay backdrop at near-white opacity.
  - Note: this is a design constraint, not a recommendation. If dark-mode support
    is added post-ingest, it requires a separate ADR decision.

## CONSTRAINT-011: DataViz SVG precision requirements
type: nfr
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-dataviz.jsx (SPEC)

Sparkline Y coordinate formula:
  y = height - ((v - min) / range) * (height - 4) - 2
  where range = max - min, clamped to 1 if zero (flat data).

MiniDonut arc formula:
  r = (size - strokeWidth) / 2
  circumference = 2 * Math.PI * r
  strokeDashoffset = circumference * (1 - Math.min(value/max, 1))
  SVG rotated -90deg globally.

These formulas must be reproduced exactly to match the design reference. Rounding
or approximation produces visual misalignment when compared to the reference HTML.

## CONSTRAINT-012: Accessibility — mandatory focus ring on all interactive elements
type: nfr
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md (PRD)
  + /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-advanced.jsx (SPEC)

Every interactive element (button, input, link, checkbox, toggle, drag handle,
pagination button, command palette item, color swatch) must show a visible focus
ring on :focus-visible. Focus ring: box-shadow var(--focus-ring). The outline
must not be removed without an equivalent visible replacement. axe-core scan must
pass with zero violations after each component ships.

## CONSTRAINT-013: TypeToConfirm guard word is "DELETE" by default
type: api-contract
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-confirmdialog.jsx (SPEC)

The confirmation target string defaults to the literal string "DELETE" (all caps).
The component must accept a prop to override this string for localization.
String comparison is case-sensitive and exact (no trim on user input — per spec).

## CONSTRAINT-014: CommandPalette keyboard event listener scope
type: protocol
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-advanced.jsx (SPEC)

The Cmd/Ctrl+K listener is attached to window (not to a container element).
The Escape listener also attached to window. Both listeners must be removed on
component unmount to prevent memory leaks. The component uses a controlled-open
pattern (open: boolean state) — the trigger button calls setOpen(true).

## Appended Ingest — 2026-05-05 (Phase 24-27 specs)

## CONSTRAINT-015: Navigation CSS class protocol
type: protocol
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-navigation.jsx (SPEC)

Adds the following classes to the cross-component class protocol (extends CONSTRAINT-007):

  ds-tree-item              tree navigation row (with .active modifier)
  ds-tree-badge             badge inside tree/sidebar item
  ds-collapsible-sidebar    collapsible sidebar container (used with .glass)
  ds-collapsible-item       row in collapsible sidebar (with .active modifier)

These must be styled in the global stylesheet to match the visual contract in
DECISION-021 through DECISION-024. CollapsibleSidebar reuses .glass surface and
.ds-icbtn for the collapse toggle.

## CONSTRAINT-016: Notification CSS class protocol
type: protocol
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-notifications.jsx (SPEC)

Adds the following classes:

  ds-notif-item             notification row in panel
  ds-notif-icon-wrap        type-tinted icon background square
  ds-notif-count            unread count badge in header
  ds-inline-banner          inline banner container (always paired with type modifier)
  ds-inline-banner-success  type modifier
  ds-inline-banner-warning  type modifier
  ds-inline-banner-error    type modifier
  ds-inline-banner-info     type modifier

Type-to-bg color tokens (used inline as RGBA per-component, not as CSS vars):
  success: rgba(34,197,94,.1)
  warning: rgba(245,158,11,.1)
  error:   rgba(239,68,68,.1)
  info:    rgba(59,130,246,.1)

These RGBA values match the vivid token equivalents (--green-vivid, --amber-vivid
implicit, --red-vivid, --blue-vivid) at 10% alpha. If a tinted-bg token system is
introduced post-ingest (e.g. --green-tint-10), it should be added via ADR.

## CONSTRAINT-017: FileUploadZone CSS class protocol
type: protocol
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-patterns.jsx (SPEC)

Adds the following classes:

  ds-upload-zone            drop zone container (with .dragging modifier on dragover)

The component reuses .glass (per-file rows), .ds-icbtn (remove buttons), and
inline styling for thumbnail and progress bar (no new classes for those — the
inline values must match DECISION-029 / DECISION-030 exactly).

## CONSTRAINT-018: NotificationCenter event handler contract
type: api-contract
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-notifications.jsx (SPEC)

State management contract — the component owns the notification array but
must accept controlled-mode props for parent ownership:

  Uncontrolled: component manages local state with initialNotifications prop
  Controlled: notifications + onNotificationsChange callback

Event handlers required:
  - onMarkAllRead(): called when "Mark all read" clicked; returns updated array
    with all read=true
  - onDismiss(id): called when per-item X clicked; returns array with id removed
  - onItemClick(id): optional; called when row body clicked (for navigation /
    drill-in); returns id

Notification shape (TypeScript-strict):
  { id: string | number, type: "success" | "warning" | "error" | "info",
    title: string, desc: string, time: string, read: boolean }

The `time` field is a pre-formatted display string (e.g. "2m ago"). Production
implementations may compute this from a Date prop using the RelativeTime component
from prior intel — but the panel itself must accept the formatted string verbatim
to remain locale-agnostic.

## CONSTRAINT-019: FileUploadZone accept and size contract
type: api-contract
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-patterns.jsx (SPEC)

The hint line in the drop zone displays "PDF, DOCX, MD · Max 10MB" in the SPEC.
This is informational text; the component must accept:
  - accept: string (file input MIME types or extensions, e.g. ".pdf,.docx,.md")
  - maxSizeMB: number (default 10)
  - hintText: string (override the displayed hint; default derives from accept + maxSizeMB)

Validation behavior is NOT specified by the SPEC and is an open implementation
decision. Recommended: reject files exceeding maxSizeMB at drop/click time and
surface an error via a parent-supplied onError(err, file) callback. This must be
locked in the component plan (Phase 26) before build.

## CONSTRAINT-020: StatusPage always-dark surface for ServerErrorPage
type: api-contract
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-status.jsx (SPEC)
  + cross-references DECISION-004 (always-dark surfaces use hardcoded values)

ServerErrorPage MUST hardcode #1c1917 (bg) and #f5f3f0 (text) — NOT var(--cream)
and var(--ink). Per DECISION-004, this prevents dark-mode from inverting both
bg AND text into cream-on-cream unreadability.

The other three status pages (NotFoundPage, MaintenancePage, OfflinePage) DO
flip with theme via standard --cream / --ink tokens. They must verify legible
contrast in both light and dark mode during axe-core scan.

This means the four StatusPages are NOT a uniform set — ServerErrorPage is a
DarkSurface context (per DECISION-004 vocabulary), the other three are flipping
Surface contexts. Component implementation must document this distinction.

## CONSTRAINT-021: StatusPage parameterization scope
type: api-contract
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-status.jsx (SPEC)

The SPEC presents pages with hardcoded copy ("This page slipped through the
cracks", "We're moving some furniture", incident ID INC-7841-A2F, etc.). For
production use, each page must accept overridable props:

  NotFoundPage:
    - eyebrow (default "Error 404 · Page not found")
    - headline (default "This page slipped through the cracks.")
    - body (default copy from SPEC)
    - primaryCta (default { label: "Back to dashboard", href: "/" })
    - secondaryCta (optional)
    - destinations (default list of common routes)
    - showReceipt (boolean, default true)

  ServerErrorPage:
    - incidentId (string, optional — controls bottom-line display)
    - serviceStatus (array of { name, state, color } for the live card)
    - statusUpdatedAt (string)
    - headline / body / primaryCta / secondaryCta (all overridable)

  MaintenancePage:
    - returnAt (Date or string)
    - countdown (live-computed { hours, minutes, seconds } from returnAt)
    - headline (default "We're moving some furniture.")
    - followLinks (array of pill links)

  OfflinePage:
    - pendingChanges (array of strings or { label, status })
    - daysCached (number, default 30, drives "last 30 days" copy)
    - onRetry (callback for "Try reconnecting")

Detailed prop contracts to be finalized in component plans (Phase 27 for
MediaCard + StatusPages). The ingest establishes the SCOPE of customization;
exact API is a per-component decision.

## CONSTRAINT-022: PlaceholderImg is a design-time primitive
type: api-contract
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-mediacards.jsx (SPEC)

PlaceholderImg renders a striped div with a centered label — NOT an actual image.
It is the design-time stand-in used inside MediaCard when no real imagery is
available. Production usage:

  <MediaCard cover={<img src="..." />} ... />

The MediaCard component must accept either:
  - A `cover` ReactNode prop (production: pass <img />, <video poster>, etc.)
  - OR fall back to PlaceholderImg with imgLabel prop (design preview / Storybook)

Per DECISION-007, the codebase ships no raster assets. PlaceholderImg's striped
diagonal pattern (repeating-linear-gradient) is CSS-only; this complies. Real
imagery in production is the consumer's responsibility — the component does not
import any image files.
